metadata.title:
HOME BUYING BIG


FEATURE Of
Ia
MARKET
sHss - - 49,

pages.raw_text:
The big feature of the local real
estate market the past month has
Been the heavy buying of small in
vestors-the home builders. While
large deals in business property
have been few, there has been a
ssteady trade in home sites and acre
tracts adjoining the city. In fact,
it is stated by many real estate
men that the volume of this kind
of business recorded the past few
weeks has been greater than at
any time in several years.


Spokane has always been home
city and the per cent of people own
ing their own homes here or buying
them on monthly instalments is
greater than other cities of the size
in the northwest. Homes and home
sites can be purchased on terms
that place them within the reach
of the wage Garner, and supplying
this demand is one of the principal
branches Of every real estate firm
of the city, although it is a business
which is not carried on with as
much brass band accompaniment
as the big trades in business and
factory property.


The hundreds and thousands of
dollars invested monthly in small
properties, lots in outlying addi-
tions, adjacent acre tracts, etc,
goes mostly unrecorded and it is
only the occasional big deal which
everybody learns about. At the
same time, when there is not big
sale to record, the small business,
the sum of which Is greater than
many big deals, is going on all the
time and forms the backbone Of the
real estate market.


1t is a noticeable fact that nearly
every sale Of this kind means i'm
provement Of the property, for few
of the buyers are spectators and
most intend to erect homes. While
several big deals mean simply
speculation, with no thought of i'm
provement on the part of the buy
ers, who are waiting for higher
values to turn a profit. the small
sale usually means new home.
The thousands OF these small deals
taken together, 1t can be seen, often
means much more to Spokane in
the building UP and improvement
of the city than the occasional big
deal.


The spring home building activity
has now opened up and there is
scarcely neighborhood where the
sound of the hammer can not be
heard in every direction. From the
number of building permits being
taken out 1t is estimated that there
will be greater activity in this line
in Spokane this year than there
was last.
